summary: Verified Seller OS Phase 4 (offer artifacts + download.html + outreach attach_artifact) and Phase 8 (retention intel score, workflows, advance). Backend pytest: Phase 4/8 = 10/10 PASS, Phase 1 = 10/10 PASS (re-verified individually). Phase 2/3 (12 tests) returned 6 ERRORS during the back-to-back full-suite run — all are 30s read-timeouts at the preview ingress when the LLM-heavy Phase 4/8 tests run immediately before them; phase 2/3 still PASS individually per iteration_9. Direct curl confirmed: POST /seller-offers/generate persists artifact with sections+score, GET /seller-offers/{id}/download.html returns text/html with title + fit-score bar + sections. Frontend Conversations renders 'View attached audit' link correctly when event has artifact_id; Retention page wires both 'Score churn risk' (POST /seller-retention/intel/score) and 'Heuristic scan' (POST /seller-retention/scan). Found ONE critical UX gap: the Send + audit / Send offer buttons on Conversations are unreachable in the normal flow.

minor.issue: Preview-edge ingress returns read-timeouts (30s) when several LLM-heavy seeds run back-to-back inside same pytest session. Not a code bug — phase 2/3 pass in isolation. Suggest bumping the requests session timeout in conftest from 30s -> 60s OR add pytest-rerunfailures.

ui_bugs.issue: 'Send + audit' and 'Send offer' buttons are GATED to lead.stage==='qualified', but the Conversations threads list (/seller-outreach/threads/{mission_id}) only returns leads with stage in {outreached, interested}. Result: buttons are ALWAYS disabled in normal flow — the only way they'd be enabled is if the lead stage is manually reverted to 'qualified'. Either (a) relax the disabled gate to allow follow-up outreach on outreached/interested leads (recommended — would let user attach an audit AFTER first cold message), or (b) include qualified leads with events in the threads list.
'Mission Control' stays highlighted purple alongside the actually-active subsection (Conversations / Retention). Pre-existing from iteration_9, still not fixed.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot validate via UI because of the gate bug above. Verified via direct API: POST /seller-outreach/generate with attach_artifact=true returns body+artifact and stamps artifact_id on the event; the Conversations page DOES render the 'View attached audit' link when an event has artifact_id, and clicking opens /seller-offers/{id}/download.html (text/html, 200, ~5KB styled HTML).

action_items: Decide whether Send + audit / Send offer buttons on Conversations should be enabled for stage in {outreached, interested}. Current behavior makes them dead UI. Recommended fix in SellerLifecycle.jsx: change disabled gate from `active.stage !== 'qualified'` to `!['qualified','outreached','interested'].includes(active.stage)` and update tooltip accordingly. The backend /seller-outreach/generate already accepts re-outreach (it just records a new event).
(Low) Fix sidebar active-route detection so 'Mission Control' is not highlighted when on a sub-page (Conversations/Retention).
(Optional) Bump pytest requests-session timeout from 30s -> 60s OR add pytest-rerunfailures so the LLM-heavy seeds in phase 2/3 do not timeout at preview ingress when run after phase 4/8.
critical_code_review_comments: SellerLifecycle.jsx is 711 lines and bundles Conversations + Retention + (likely) Discovery — split into focused subcomponents per route per file-size guideline (>700 lines).
/seller-outreach/threads/{mission_id} hard-codes the stage allow-list ['outreached','interested']; consider exposing as a query param (?stages=) for richer UX.

openArtifact() in Conversations uses window.open with onClick — works, but the rendered element therefore lacks an href attribute, so middle-click / right-click 'open in new tab' / copy-link does not work. Render an <a href={`${API}/seller-offers/${id}/download.html`} target="_blank" rel="noopener"> instead.
Pytest tests do not protect against preview-edge slowness; recommend retry-on-timeout for setup helpers like _seed_qualified_lead.

success_rate.backend: 100% (Phase 1: 10/10, Phase 4/8: 10/10) — Phase 2/3 12 tests pass individually but flake under back-to-back run
success_rate.frontend: 85% (1 HIGH UX gate bug, 1 LOW sidebar highlight)

test_credentials: Session: test_session_1779636592168 (user_test1779636592168, is_admin=true). Cookie + Bearer both work.
seed_data_creation: Created and CLEANED UP: mission 34efcea1d6b945f6aa39a62f295093f6 + lead ac360b8d992b42fc8afb16c539c422d7 + outreach event cf54ec1dfead4dceaadd0b5f7e49b741 + artifact d9f8d10e5a954a948e0b489790b4fcea. All deleted at end of run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 4 (download.html, attach_artifact end-to-end) and Phase 8 (intel score + workflows) BACKEND is solid. The blocker is purely a frontend gating bug in SellerLifecycle.jsx Conversations panel — the disabled condition `active.stage !== 'qualified'` makes Send/Send+audit unreachable because threads only show outreached/interested. To retest after fix: (1) seed qualified lead with at least 1 outreach event (manually mongo-insert), or use the test-credentials user and run /seller-discovery/run + /seller-qualification/qualify + /seller-outreach/generate to land at outreached, (2) open Conversations, click thread, click Send + audit, (3) wait ~70s for LLM, (4) confirm a new sent event appears with artifact_id and 'View attached audit' link, (5) click link -> /seller-offers/{id}/download.html opens with text/html. Sidebar active-state is a separate small fix.
rca of the issue: Conversations Send/Audit disabled-gate bug: SellerLifecycle.jsx renders the active-thread side with the buttons disabled when active.stage !== 'qualified'. The list of selectable threads is fetched from /api/seller-outreach/threads/{mission_id}, which filters seller_leads to stage in {outreached, interested}. The intersection is EMPTY, so buttons can never be enabled from normal navigation. Reproduction: (1) seed a qualified lead, (2) send first outreach (advances stage to outreached), (3) open Conversations — the lead appears, click thread — both buttons are disabled with tooltip 'Outreach disabled — lead is outreached'. Mitigation: enable the buttons for stage in {qualified, outreached, interested} (re-outreach is a valid use case and backend already supports it).
